game_info.concept: Plague Inc. Plague Inc. is a strategy simulation where players evolve a pathogen to infect and eradicate humanity before a cure is developed, spending "DNA points" to upgrade transmission methods, symptoms, and resistances. The game simulates global infection spread on a world map, factoring in country responses, research efforts, and public awareness, with difficulty levels impacting cure speed and starting conditions.
game_info.description: In Pandemic Spread, you control a deadly virus with the goal of infecting the entire world before a cure is developed. Spend DNA points to evolve your virus with new transmission methods, symptoms, and resistances. Watch as your infection spreads across countries while scientists race to develop a cure. Balance making your virus deadly enough to win while keeping it stealthy enough to avoid accelerating cure research.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate between evolution options
Space: Select/purchase evolution
Z: Toggle information view (shows detailed country stats)
Enter: Start game from title screen
Esc: Pause/unpause game
R: Restart game when on game over screen
game_info.automated_testing:
<TEST_1>
<test_description>Tests basic game functionality and responsiveness using randomized sticky key inputs to ensure the game runs without errors.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Cycles through different key inputs at random intervals to simulate player interaction with all game systems.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>Game should continue running without crashes, responding appropriately to inputs and maintaining consistent framerate.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_1>
<TEST_2>
<test_description>Tests that the game can be won by efficiently evolving the virus to infect all countries before the cure is completed.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Strategically purchases transmission upgrades first, followed by resistances and symptoms in an optimal sequence.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>All countries should become infected and the player should reach the win screen before the cure progress reaches 100%.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_2>
<TEST_3>
<test_description>Tests that the game can be lost if poor evolution choices are made, allowing the cure to be developed.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Makes deliberately suboptimal choices, focusing too early on high visibility symptoms without proper transmission methods.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>The cure should reach 100% completion before all countries are infected, resulting in the game over lose screen.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_3>
<TEST_4>
<test_description>Tests game balance by attempting to win with minimal DNA point spending.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Purchases only the most essential upgrades and hoards DNA points, testing if minimal strategy can still succeed.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>The test should demonstrate whether the game is properly balanced, with minimal upgrades making winning challenging but possible.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_4>
<TEST_5>
<test_description>Tests that the information toggle system works correctly and provides useful data to the player.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Frequently toggles between information views while playing to verify all country and global statistics are displayed correctly.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>Information views should toggle properly and display accurate, up-to-date statistics about infection rates and cure progress.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_5>
